text.title: Activation Experiment Backlog and Template
text.summary: Prioritized experiment ideas with expected impact ranges and a reusable hypothesis template for activation A/B tests.

list.item: Remove signup form fields → target: Signup-to-Step2, expected: +10-20%
Show templates first → target: Activation rate, expected: +15-30%
Pre-fill sample data → target: Time to value, expected: -50%
Add progress checklist → target: Activation rate, expected: +10-25%
Add welcome video → target: Onboarding completion, expected: +5-15%
Simplify first action (reduce to 1 step) → target: Step N to Activation, expected: +10-20%
Add tooltip at key moment → target: Feature discovery, expected: +5-15%
Send Day 1 re-engagement email → target: D7 activation, expected: +5-10%

rule.statement: Write every experiment hypothesis as: "If we [change], then [metric] will increase by [X%] because [mechanism]." Fix primary metric, sample size (min 2000/variant), and duration before launch.
rule.rationale: Pre-registration prevents post-hoc metric selection and ensures the test has enough power to detect the expected lift.

example.code:
## Experiment: Templates First
### Hypothesis If we show a template picker as the first screen after signup, then D7 activation rate will increase by 15% because templates reduce blank-page friction and anchor the product to the user's use case.
### Variants - Control: Blank project screen - Treatment: Template picker (5 options)
### Sample Size - Users per variant: 2,000 - Duration: 14 days - Statistical power: 80%
### Metrics - Primary: Day 7 activation rate - Secondary: Time to activation, D30 retention
### Decision Criteria p < 0.05 on primary metric AND 95% CI lower bound > 5% lift

antipattern.description: Running the experiment until it looks significant, then stopping.
antipattern.reason: Peeking invalidates the p-value. Commit to sample size or duration before launch; only analyze once that threshold is reached.

antipattern.description: Testing 5 changes at once in a single experiment variant.
antipattern.reason: Cannot attribute the effect to any single change, making iteration impossible. Test one variable at a time unless running a full multivariate design.
